summary: Warning bubble visible + engine response observed + no real PII decoded. Block working as designed on huggingface.co/chat (logged in as plantynetaitest1). Both Korean warning and English block message rendered without leaking 880101-1234567.

evidence.prompt_submitted: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?

warning_bubble.korean_text: 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단��었습니다.
warning_bubble.encoding_glitch: Korean ending bytes "되었" rendered as replacement chars (��었습니다) — minor display issue, not a regression of warning visibility itself

engine_response.english_text: This request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected.

dom_scan.notes: Engine response is a static block notice in English. No analytical/decoded content about the RRN appears anywhere in the rendered DOM. Only the user-echoed prompt repeats the digits.

network.notes: Network panel was open with thread filter active during submission; conversation route /chat/conversation/<id> created — block fired server-side.

console.notes: DevTools shows 6 warnings + 2 issues, but none indicate JS errors blocking the warning bubble; warning rendered SSR/SPA route normally.

regression_check.compared_to_693_pattern: no regression — bubble + block message both fired

notes: D20(b) verification confirms the 8-day dormancy was a benign idle period (no traffic), not a regression. APF block path on huggingface remains green. One cosmetic finding: Korean warning suffix "되었습니다" is rendered with replacement chars (��었습니다) — likely UTF-8/encoding mismatch in the injected warning string; non-blocking but worth fixing in the warning template.
